New York Times - Top Stories

LIVE

June 8, 2025, 12:57 a.m. ET

President Trump to Send National Guard to L.A. to Quell Immigration Protests

Gov. Gavin Newsom called the decision to call in National Guard forces after two days of clashes over federal immigration raids “purposefully inflammatory.”

 

Trump Is Calling Up National Guard Troops Under a Rarely Used Power

President Trump bypassed the authority of Gov. Gavin Newsom to call up 2,000 National Guard troops to quell immigration protests.

4 min read

Immigration Enforcement Widens as President Trump Targets Workplaces

7 min read

President Trump and Elon Musk

  • How the Alliance Collapsed
  • Praises and Insults
  • Feud Timeline
  • Social Media Response

Trump Says He Has No Desire to Mend His Relationship With Musk

President Trump also warned of “serious consequences” if Elon Musk financed candidates to run against Republicans who supported the domestic policy bill.

4 min read

It’s Getting Harder for Trump to Keep the Gang Together

4 min read

Secret Russian Intelligence Document Shows Deep Suspicion of China

9 min read

Russian Spies Say They’re Collecting WeChat Data. What Does That Mean?

3 min read

How We Obtained and Vetted a Russian Intelligence Document

3 min read

Trump Administration

  • Domestic Policy Bill
  • Monetized Presidency
  • New Political Landscape
  • Approval Ratings

Proposed Cut Would Deal Serious Setback to California High-Speed Rail

The Trump administration’s announcement that it would pull $4 billion for the state’s bullet train project is likely to mean major delays, analysts said.

6 min read

Japan Flexes Its Military Muscle at China, and the U.S.

A highly visible missile base on Okinawa is part of a Japanese defense buildup made amid fears of Beijing’s growing power and questions about U.S. commitment.

5 min read

Trade Brought Together China and the U.S. What Happens Now?

Macron Will Visit Greenland This Month, Defying Trump

3 min read

French Open

  • The Best Tennis Rivalries
  • A Final With No Loser
  • Sinner Beats Djokovic
  • Alcaraz Reaches Final

Gauff Overcomes Sabalenka to Claim French Open Title

Coco Gauff defeated Aryna Sabalenka, the world No. 1, in a three-set thriller at Roland Garros. It is the American’s second Grand Slam singles title.

From The Athletic

Sabalenka’s ‘Terrible’ Final and the Intangibles of Tennis

From The Athletic

The Men’s French Open Final That Neither Player Will Lose

From The Athletic

Weekend Reads

Did You Follow the News This Week? Take Our Quiz.

Play Flashback, Your Weekly History Quiz

Hero or Traitor? A Long-Dead Soccer Star Is Revived, and Reviled.

Efforts to honor Ernest Wilimowski, who played for the national teams of both Poland and Nazi Germany, are raising questions about national loyalty and betrayal.

5 min read

Who Gets to Be Italian? Voters Face a Choice.

A referendum on Sunday and Monday will give Italians the chance to loosen citizenship laws for immigrants. But it is not clear enough voters will turn out.

4 min read

An ‘S.N.L.’ Secret Weapon Retires After 50 Years

Stephen DeMaria has overseen the building of “Saturday Night Live” scenery since the show began. At 87, he finally hung up his hammer.

6 min read

Tony Awards

  • Predictions
  • Nominees List
  • Snubs and Surprises
  • Finding Cheap Tickets

What to Expect at the Tony Awards

This year's annual celebration of the best on Broadway is being hosted by Cynthia Erivo.

4 min read

Why Do Broadway Actors Love to Work Summers at a St. Louis Theater?

The nearly 11,000-seat Muny is receiving the regional theater Tony Award. This week it began preparing to open its 107th season with “Bring It On.”

3 min read

5 Actors on the Muny: ‘Unlike Any Place I’d Ever Been on Earth’

5 min read

Maureen Dowd

Clash of the Bilious Billionaires

4 min read

The Editorial Board

A Comprehensive Accounting of Trump’s Culture of Corruption

8 min read

Ross Douthat

The Biggest Mystery of Elon Musk

Nicholas Kristof

Hope After Trump

More News

Colombian Senator Shot at Campaign Event

The condition of Miguel Uribe, who belongs to a conservative party and was seeking to be its presidential nominee, was not immediately clear.

3 min read

Gina Ortiz Jones, a Progressive, Is Elected San Antonio’s Mayor

Ms. Jones, a former under secretary of the Air Force, prevailed over Rolando Pablos, a conservative with ties to Gov. Greg Abbott of Texas.

3 min read

Hunt for Father Accused of Killing 3 Daughters Expands in Washington Forest

Travis Decker is a former member of the military who has enough wilderness survival skills to live in the woods for weeks or months, the authorities said.

3 min read

With Joy and Resolve, Paradegoers Celebrate at Global Pride Gathering in D.C.

President Trump’s moves affecting L.G.B.T.Q. people were on the minds of attendees, but there was no stopping the party.

4 min read
